the training

You weren't born unsure of yourself. You were taught to be.

Somewhere between being a good girl and being a good woman, most of us learned to outsource the one job that was always ours: knowing what we actually think, want, and need.

01

You learned to check with everyone else first, before you'd even let yourself have an opinion.

02

You got praised for being easy, agreeable, low-maintenance, so you kept becoming that, at your own expense.

03

You started calling your instincts "overthinking" instead of what they were: information.

04

And then you were blamed for the very thing you were trained to do: not trusting yourself.

You're not broken. You're just disconnected from yourself.

this is not another self-help app

It won't fix everything overnight, and it's not therapy. It's a practice: a place to catch yourself mid-abandonment and choose differently, one decision at a time.

how it works

Tell her what you're stuck on. She already knows what's underneath it.

01

Name the decision

Type out whatever you're stuck on: a job, a boundary, a "should I or shouldn't I."

02

Hear her reflect it back

Your higher self names what you're actually weighing, not the surface story, the real one.

03

See what it revealed

A clear, short takeaway you can act on, not a lecture, not a worksheet.

Each time you hear it in your own words, it gets a little harder to believe the old story.

the app

A place to practice trusting yourself again.

She already knows what she wants.

Decision support for the moments you'd normally ask everyone but yourself.

She already knows her patterns.

Gentle reflection that helps you catch self-abandonment before it happens again.

She already knows her worth.

A reflection that separates your value from your output, right when you need to hear it.

She already knows how to come home.

A steady practice for choosing yourself again, one moment at a time.

None of this came from a textbook. Here's who's actually behind it.

the person behind it

I built this because I needed it first.

Here's what happened: I spent years being the good girl. A military spouse, a mom of two, doing everything I thought I was supposed to do, and still asking everyone else what I should do before I ever let myself have an opinion.

For years I thought the problem was that no one understood me. It turned out I'd never understood myself.

I used to be so unkind to myself. Resentful, because I felt misunderstood, and it seemed like no one considered how I felt the way I considered how they felt. Every disappointment just confirmed what I already believed about myself: that I wasn't enough.

So I started paying attention to the exact moments I abandoned myself, and built a tool to catch it before it happened again. One video, one decision, one story at a time, I let go of the shame that used to define me and started sharing what I'd actually been through. 535 followers became 59,000. Tamron Hall's team reached out. I spoke to 10,000+ people in a virtual masterclass about content.

But none of it was ever actually the point. The practice underneath it, hearing myself and choosing myself over fear, is the exact thing this app is built to help you do. It won't grow your following or write your content. It's built for the part that came before any of that: the decision to actually listen to yourself.

I'm not a therapist. I'm not a guru.

I'm a woman who spent years abandoning herself. The one who built the tool she needed first, and hoped she wasn't the only one.

"I didn't create this platform to give people answers. I created it to help them rediscover the ones they've carried within them all along."
Kayla Pattle · founder, She Already Knows

read this before you start

Is this for you?

this is for you if
  • You second-guess decisions you already know the answer to.
  • You ask everyone else before you ever ask yourself.
  • You want a space to think out loud without being told what to do.
  • You're tired of "just trust yourself" advice with no way to practice it.
this isn't for you if
  • You're looking for a licensed therapist or crisis support.
  • You want someone else to make the decision for you.
  • You're not willing to sit with a question before getting an answer.

questions

Before you start.

is this therapy?

No. She Already Knows isn't a replacement for therapy or medical care. It's a daily practice for decision-making and self-trust. Think of it as a mirror, not a clinician.

how is this different from chatgpt?

ChatGPT can answer literally anything, and that's kind of the problem. She Already Knows only does one thing: reflect your own thinking back to you, in a style built specifically around self-trust and self-abandonment patterns, not generic advice. No more spending hours prompt-crafting to get something useful out of it. You just say what you're stuck on.

what happens after my free response?

You get one reflection completely free, no account needed. If you want to keep going, you'll create an account, and that's when your 7-day free trial starts. You're not charged until day 8.

can i cancel anytime?

Yes. Cancel during your trial and you won't be charged anything. Cancel any time after and you won't be billed for the next month.

is what i type private?

This isn't a public feed or a community forum. You're not posting for anyone else to see or respond to. Full details on how your responses are handled are in our terms, which you'll agree to when you create an account.

what if i only use it sometimes?

That's normal. This isn't about perfect daily use. It's here for the moments you actually need it, whenever those come up.
